title_guess: Naseby, Rushton Triangular Lodge & Little Staughton (with Charles)

captions: Rushton Triangle Lodge 1593. Built by [?Tresham] RC. after being freed from prison.
pages.text:
Saturday June 20th.
4 of the 5 boys are moving house this summer. This week John is holidaying in L.A. William has just moved into the farm. Charles is storing stuff in our garage before moving into a room while doing up his new house. Peter moves to London next week. John moves [from]

[Aerial photograph of a triangular building with surrounding grounds]

[Rotated caption beside photo:] Rushton Triangle Lodge 1593. Built by [?Tresham] RC. after being freed from prison.

Swansea to Cardiff.
        At. 10.30. Simon, Eve Charles & Tinder left for Naseby. It's a good viewpoint.
Prince [?Charles] [strikethrough: Rupert] cavalry chased the New Army cavalry away & the Cromwell [?'s] infantry killed the Cavalier infantry.
        Then on to the Triangular Lodge.
A most odd, small building built by RC. He was the grandfather of a gunpowder plotter, married to a Throckmorton.
        I had had a sore throat for 2 nights before the trip + suddenly at Naseby it became a cold. When we got to Top End*** campsite at Little Staughton, I stayed inside while Simon + Charles sat outside + walked. Half the site is given over to a Ford Corsair rally. Very serious business. Simon + Charles are spending much time discussing Morgan engineering.
        We had a quiet night.
